items.correct: Meaning conveyed beyond what is literally said — generated when speakers obey or deliberately flout Grice's maxims. 'Some students passed' implicates 'not all', via the maxim of quantity.
items.misconception: Implicature is the same as the literal meaning of a sentence.
items.random_wrong: Implicature is a kind of grammatical mistake.
items.why: Implicature is precisely the gap between said and meant. Students conflate literal and intended meaning, missing why pragmatics is needed at all — much of communication is implied, not stated.

items.q: What is the difference between descriptive and prescriptive approaches to language?
items.correct: Descriptivists describe how language is actually used; prescriptivists rule on how language ought to be used — modern linguistics is descriptivist.
items.misconception: Descriptivists describe written language and prescriptivists describe spoken language.
items.random_wrong: Both terms mean the same — analysing language scientifically.
items.why: The split is about attitude, not mode. Descriptivists treat all varieties as valid; prescriptivists make value judgements. Students mistake it for a written/spoken distinction.

items.q: What is Skinner's behaviourist view of language acquisition?
items.correct: Children learn language through imitation and operant conditioning — they are reinforced for correct utterances and corrected for errors.
items.misconception: Skinner believed in an innate grammar module.
items.random_wrong: Skinner believed children couldn't learn language until age five.
items.why: Skinner is the BEHAVIOURIST — the opposite of nativists like Chomsky. Students mix up the two. Skinner's view is environmental; Chomsky's is innate.
It's Noun. “Verb” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The doer of the verb is the.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Subject. “Object” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In 'She opened the door', how many words have a word class?
items.why: Exact answer: 4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
